theme_and_archetype.reasoning: UPSC preparation demands high focus, authority, and trust. The Deep Blue (#1A365D) establishes academic prestige, while the Gold (#D69E2E) signifies achievement. Clean whites/grays prevent cognitive overload in data-heavy tracking dashboards.

button.primary: bg-[#1A365D] text-white rounded-xl py-4 px-6 font-semibold flex flex-row justify-center items-center active:opacity-90
button.secondary: bg-white border-2 border-[#1A365D] text-[#1A365D] rounded-xl py-4 px-6 font-semibold flex flex-row justify-center items-center active:bg-slate-50
button.accent: bg-[#D69E2E] text-white rounded-xl py-4 px-6 font-bold shadow-md flex flex-row justify-center items-center

card.default: bg-white rounded-2xl p-5 shadow-[0_4px_12px_rgba(0,0,0,0.05)] border border-slate-100
card.metric: bg-white rounded-2xl p-4 border border-slate-100 flex flex-col gap-1

input.default: bg-slate-50 border border-slate-200 rounded-lg px-4 py-3 text-base text-slate-900 focus:border-[#1A365D] focus:bg-white

status_badges.green: bg-emerald-100 text-emerald-700 px-2.5 py-1 rounded-full text-xs font-bold
status_badges.yellow: bg-amber-100 text-amber-700 px-2.5 py-1 rounded-full text-xs font-bold
status_badges.red: bg-rose-100 text-rose-700 px-2.5 py-1 rounded-full text-xs font-bold

auth.layout: Full-screen image background using the auth_background asset. Add a bottom-heavy white card overlay (rounded-t-3xl) for the Google Login button and branding.
auth.features: Prominent 'Login with Google' CTA. High contrast, clean typography.

student_dashboard.layout: Vertical scroll layout. Top header with Greeting + Profile Avatar. Followed by a row of Metric Cards (Tasks Done, Hours). Next: 'My Mentor' card (Image + Contact CTA). Next: Study Tracker (Ring chart or Progress bar). Next: Upcoming Tasks list.
student_dashboard.cards: Use the 'highlight' card variant (Deep Blue bg) for the immediate 'Next Task' to draw attention.

mentor_dashboard.layout: Top Tabs for switching between 'My Mentees', 'Schedule', 'Analytics'. List view of mentees using the traffic light badges (green/yellow/red) to convey progress instantly.
mentor_dashboard.interactions: Tapping a mentee row opens a detailed modal or screen for adding feedback/assigning tasks.

admin_dashboard.layout: Side drawer navigation (on tablet) or top tabs (on mobile). Start with a grid of metric cards. Below, use data tables/lists for User Management and Content Management.
admin_dashboard.style: High density, strict grid layout. Follow Archetype 4 Swiss style data representation. Every pixel utilized efficiently.

auth_background.description: Abstract minimalist background for auth screens, deep navy with gold accents.

achievement_icon.description: 3D golden trophy for empty states, task completions, or achievement modals.

student_avatars.description: Student studying (Use for placeholder/demo student profile)
Students collaborating (Use for community or group session placeholders)

icons.instructions: Use lucide-react-native for all app icons to ensure cross-platform consistency. Use 24px default size, 2px stroke width.

instructions_to_main_agent: This is a React Native Expo application. Adapt the provided Tailwind classes using NativeWind or standard React Native StyleSheet equivalents.
Implement a Bottom Tab Navigator for the Student App, using Lucide icons. For Mentors/Admins, use Top Tabs or a Drawer based on the platform.
Data visualization components (Progress bars, Pie charts) MUST be rendered using React Native SVG or a charting library like react-native-chart-kit / victory-native.
Always use 'SafeAreaView' or safe area insets to prevent UI overlap with notches and home indicators.
Ensure all touchable elements (Buttons, Tab items) have a minimum height of 44px for thumb-friendly interaction.
CRITICAL TESTING RULE: All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a 'testID' attribute (the React Native equivalent of data-testid) in kebab-case, e.g., testID='login-google-button'.
Auth Screen: Implement an ImageBackground using the 'auth_background' URL. Overlay a View positioned at the bottom containing the Google Auth button.
Mentor Dashboard: Strictly follow the traffic light coloring (green/yellow/red) for mentee progress to allow instant triage of students.
